title: croton gratissimus var. subgratissimus
intro: Croton gratissimus var. subgratissimus, also known as the 'Tread-softly' or 'Makwa' plant, is a variant of the Croton gratissimus species, a type of succulent shrub native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Croton gratissimus var. subgratissimus include its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for its medicinal uses, with the leaves and stems being the primary parts used in herbal preparations.
